popularStory.headline: History of Eleanor of England (1161-1214)
popularStory.description: Eleanor of England (Spanish: Leonor; c.1161 – 31 October 1214), was Queen of Castile and Toledo[3] as wife of Alfonso VIII of Castile. She was the sixth child and second daughter of Henry II, King of …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
John (24 December 1166 – 19 October 1216) was King of England from 1199 until his death in 1216. He lost the Duchy of Normandy and most of his other French lands to King Philip II of France, resulting in the collapse of the Angevin Empire and contributing to the subsequent growth in power of the French Capetian dynasty during the 13th century. The baronial revolt at the end of John's reign led to the sealing of Magna Carta, a document considered an early step in the evolution of the constitution of the United Kingdom.
John was the youngest of the four surviving sons of King Henry II of England and Duchess Eleanor of Aquitaine. He was nicknamed John Lackland because he was not expected to inherit significant lands.
...
John was made Count of Mortain, was married to the wealthy Isabella of Gloucester, and was given valuable lands in Lancaster and the counties of Cornwall, Derby, Devon, Dorset, Nottingham and Somerset, all with the aim of buying his loyalty to Richard whilst the King was on crusade.
...
John's decision in August 1200 to marry Isabella of Angoulême. In order to remarry, John first needed to abandon his wife Isabella, Countess of Gloucester; the King accomplished this by arguing that he had failed to get the necessary papal dispensation to marry the Countess in the first place—as a cousin, John could not have legally wedded her without this.
...
John's personal life greatly affected his reign. Contemporary chroniclers state that John was sinfully lustful and lacking in piety. It was common for kings and nobles of the period to keep mistresses, but chroniclers complained that John's mistresses were married noblewomen, which was considered unacceptable. John had at least five children with mistresses during his first marriage, and two of those mistresses are known to have been noblewomen. John's behaviour after his second marriage is less clear, however. None of his known illegitimate children were born after he remarried, and there is no actual documentary proof of adultery after that point
...

John and Isabella of Angoulême had five children:

1. Henry III, King of England (1 October 1207 – 16 November 1272)
2. Richard, King of the Romans (5 January 1209 – 2 April 1272)
3. Joan, Queen of Scotland (22 July 1210 – 4 March 1238)
4. Isabella, Holy Roman Empress (1214 – 1 December 1241)
5. Eleanor, Countess of Pembroke (1215 – 13 April 1275)

John had more than ten known illegitimate children, of whom the best known are:

1. Richard FitzRoy (c. 1190 – June 1246) whose mother was Adela, John's first cousin
2. Joan, Lady of Wales (c. 1191 – February 1237) also known by her Welsh name of Siwan

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John,_King_of_England

------------------------------------------------------------------

Timeline for King John
Year Event
1199 John accedes to the throne on the death of his brother, Richard I.
1204 England loses most of its possessions in France.
1205 John refuses to accept Stephen Langton as Archbishop of Canterbury
1208 Pope Innocent III issues an Interdict against England, banning all church services except baptisms and funerals
1209 Pope Innocent III excommunicates John for his confiscation of ecclesiastical property
1209 Cambridge University founded
1212 Innocent III declares that John is no longer the rightful King
1213 John submits to the Pope’s demands and accepts the authority of the Pope
1214 Philip Augustus of France defeats the English at the Battle of Bouvines
1215 Beginning of the Barons' war. The English Barons march to London to demand rights which they lay down in the Magna Carta.
1215 John meets the English barons at Runnymede, agrees to their demands, and seals the Magna Carta which set limits on the powers of the monarch, lays out the feudal obligations of the barons, confirms the liberties of the Church, and grants rights to all freemen of the realm and their heirs for ever. It is the first written constitution.
1215 The Pope decrees that John need not adhere to the Magna Carta, and civil war breaks out
1216 The barons seek French aid in their fight against John. Prince Louis of France lands in England and captures the Tower of London
1216 John flees North and loses his war chest of cash and jewels in the Wash estuary
1216 John dies of a fever at Newark and is buried Worcester Cathedral

zh.lifeSummary: 當John King of England於1166年12月24日出生在英國奧可福爾奧可福德時，他的父親Henry II King of England33歲，母親Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre44歲。他於1188年8月24日與Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester結婚。他去世於1216年10月19日在英國諾廷加姆爾內瓦爾可--特倫特享年49歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When John King of England was born on 24 December 1166, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Henry II King of England, was 33 and his mother, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, was 44. He married Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester on 24 August 1188. He died on 19 October 1216, in Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John King of England est né le 24 décembre 1166, à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry II King of England, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, avait 44 ans. Il a épousé Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester le 24 août 1188. Il est décédé le 19 octobre 1216, à Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John King of England am 24. Dezember 1166 in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry II King of England 33 und seine Mutter Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre 44. Er hat am 24. August 1188 Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester geheiratet. Er starb am 19. Oktober 1216 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England und wurde in Worcester, Worcestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John King of England è nato il il 24 dicembre 1166 nel Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, il suo padre, Henry II King of England, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, aveva 44 anni. Lui sposato Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester il 24 agosto 1188. Morì il 19 ottobre 1216, nel Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Worcester, Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John King of Englandが1166年12月24日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォードで生まれた時，父Henry II King of Englandは33歳，母Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterreは44歳であった。1188年8月24日にIsabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucesterと結婚した。彼は1216年10月19日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアニューアルク-オン-トレントで49歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John King of England(이)가 1166년 12월 24일 영국 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry II King of England(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre(은)는 44세였다. 그는 1188년 8월 24일 Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1216년 10월 19일 영국 노틴그함실르 뉴아르k--트렌트에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John King of England nasceu em 24 de dezembro de 1166, em Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry II King of England, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, tinha 44 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester em 24 de agosto de 1188. Ele faleceu em 19 de outubro de 1216, em Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John King of England родился 24 декабря 1166 г. в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry II King of England было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella FitzWilliam Countess of Gloucester 24 августа 1188 г. Он умер 19 октября 1216 г. в местности нюарк-он-трент, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия.
